text:
Congratulations @SpaceX team on an epic first Starship V3 launch & landing!

You scored a goal for humanity.
summary: Congratulations @SpaceX team on an epic first Starship V3 launch & landing! You scored a goal for humanity.
ai_summary: Elon Musk congratulates the SpaceX team on successfully completing the first Starship V3 launch and landing, marking a significant engineering milestone for the company's fully reusable super-heavy lift vehicle program. The post highlights SpaceX's progress in iterative rocket development and the technical achievement of recovering and landing the vehicle, which is critical to the company's long-term goal of reducing launch costs and enabling sustainable space exploration and Mars colonization.
embed_html:
Congratulations @SpaceX team on an epic first Starship V3 launch & landing!

You scored a goal for humanity.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 23, 2026

text:
The hydraulic pin holding the tower arm in place did not retract. 

If that can be fixed tonight, there will be another launch attempt tomorrow at 5:30 CT. https://t.co/DJAdvDYQpH
summary: The hydraulic pin holding the tower arm in place did not retract. If that can be fixed tonight, there will be another launch attempt tomorrow at 5:30 CT.
ai_summary: Elon Musk reports a technical failure during a SpaceX launch attempt: a hydraulic pin on the tower arm failed to retract, necessitating repairs before the next launch window. This demonstrates real-time operational transparency on critical engineering challenges in rocket launches and highlights how infrastructure issues (not just rocket systems) can delay complex aerospace operations—a key lesson for anyone scaling hardware-dependent businesses where supply chain and mechanical systems create cascading dependencies.
embed_html:
The hydraulic pin holding the tower arm in place did not retract.

If that can be fixed tonight, there will be another launch attempt tomorrow at 5:30 CT. https://t.co/DJAdvDYQpH

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 21, 2026

text: Starship V3 first flight countdown starting https://t.co/6UL2FEZ6Bb
summary: Starship V3 first flight countdown starting
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced the countdown for Starship V3's first flight, indicating SpaceX's progress on its next-generation super heavy-lift launch vehicle. This represents a critical engineering and product development milestone for SpaceX's reusable rocket program, demonstrating advancement in the company's iterative approach to rapid prototyping and deployment of increasingly capable rocket systems for commercial and governmental space missions.
embed_html:
Starship V3 first flight countdown starting https://t.co/6UL2FEZ6Bb

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 21, 2026

parent.text: Just wanna say, look at that heat shield. This has to be the cleanest one yet post-entry.
parent.name: Mookafish
parent.embed_html:
Just wanna say, look at that heat shield. This has to be the cleanest one yet post-entry. pic.twitter.com/XidAnD8YBw

— Mookafish (@Mookafish) May 22, 2026

ai_summary: Mookafish observes that SpaceX's latest heat shield shows exceptional post-entry condition with no burn-throughs, and Musk confirms the shield performed as designed. This reflects a critical engineering milestone in SpaceX's reusable spacecraft program, demonstrating iterative progress on thermal protection systems that are essential for reliable, cost-effective spacecraft recovery and reusability—a key technical hurdle in advancing commercial space operations.
embed_html:
No burn-throughs. Shield held.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) May 23, 2026
